items.headline: City launches business license amnesty program for local shops
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help businesses get compliant with licensing requirements, with outreach targeted at shopping centers. This could affect local shop owners who may have fallen behind on city licensing.

items.headline: New incentive programs aim to attract retail and startups to Cupertino
items.summary: The city is rolling out economic development incentives designed to draw retail businesses and startups to the area. This signals a push to diversify Cupertino's economy beyond tech.

items.headline: New mixed-use housing development approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading. This brings new housing to the area, though details on unit count and affordability were not specified in the agenda.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state prohousing funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for California's Prohousing Incentive Program (Round 4) to help accelerate housing development. This funding could support zoning changes or streamlined approvals to increase local housing supply.

items.headline: City seeks public input on $1M+ federal housing funds allocation
items.summary: Santa Clara is holding a public hearing on March 24 to gather community feedback on how to spend HUD grant money for housing programs in the 2026-27 fiscal year. Residents can comment on priorities for affordable housing, homelessness services, and community development.

items.headline: City hosting community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale held a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This program typically addresses vehicle dwelling and homelessness by providing designated parking areas for people living in cars.
